target.name: Alef Aeronautics
target.founder: Jim Dukhovny, Constantine Kisly, Pavel Markin, Oleg Petrov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Alef Aeronautics is a Series A transportation company founded by Jim Dukhovny, Constantine Kisly, Pavel Markin, and Oleg Petrov. The company has raised $30M+ to date. The Transportation sector contains 8 private peer companies, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and FleetZero, though no direct public comparables exist.

Stage-progression valuation is the applicable methodology here. Using total raised ($30M) as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $60M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable datapoints within the narrow Transportation filter (fewer than three qualifying matches), making sector-wide medians unreliable.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M to $69M, with a median of $60M. This range reflects limited methodological convergence—only one method is defensible given data constraints. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x stage multiplier for this specific subsector, the representativeness of $30M+ raised as a valuation proxy absent a current round price, and the absence of tightly matched peers. Any refinement would require additional comparable transaction data within the frontier transportation space or disclosure of recent financing terms.
